stories.title: Bobby Witt Jr. Exits With Right Knee Injury — Royals Await MRI Results
stories.angle: Royals All-Star shortstop Bobby Witt Jr. left Thursday's 14-6 win over the Cardinals in the fourth inning with right knee discomfort after making a sliding stop on a ground ball. He hit a home run before exiting. It is the same knee that sidelined him briefly on June 7. Manager Matt Quatraro said MRI results pending. Witt is the heart of the Kansas City lineup and an MVP-caliber player — any extended absence would fundamentally change the Royals' second-half trajectory.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
⚠️ Bobby Witt Jr. exits Royals-Cardinals with right knee discomfort.

Left in the 4th inning after a sliding stop. Hit a HR before leaving.

Same knee that gave him trouble June 7. Manager says MRI pending.

Royals won 14-6. But the only story that matters is No. 7. #Royals

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Witt Jr. knee history:

June 7: right knee soreness vs. Twins. Returned 2 days later. Manager said nothing structurally wrong.

June 18: right knee discomfort again vs. Cardinals. MRI pending.

He's a top-5 AL player. KC can't afford this. #Royals

article.headline: Bobby Witt Jr. Exits With Right Knee Injury — Royals Await MRI Results

stories.title: Schwarber Becomes First to 25 Home Runs in 2026
stories.angle: Kyle Schwarber hit his 25th home run to become the first player in the major leagues to reach that mark in 2026. He is batting .249 with 35 extra-base hits, 43 RBI, 46 walks, and 44 runs across 315 plate appearances. His homer pace projects to approximately 66 home runs for the season — a number that would be the third-highest single-season total in MLB history, behind only Barry Bonds (73) and Mark McGwire (70). The Home Run Derby in July is set for Citizens Bank Park, Schwarber's home ballpark.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Kyle Schwarber: first player in the majors to hit 25 home runs in 2026.

.249 BA. 43 RBI. 46 walks. 315 PAs.

Pace: ~66 HR. That's the 3rd-highest single-season total in MLB history.

And his Home Run Derby is at Citizens Bank Park — his house. #Phillies

article.headline: Schwarber First to 25 Home Runs in 2026 — Historic Pace Continues

stories.title: Bryan Woo Dominates in Seattle — Mariners Blank Orioles 3-0
stories.angle: Seattle Mariners right-hander Bryan Woo pitched seven shutout innings, allowing just three hits and striking out five, in a 3-0 win over the Baltimore Orioles on Thursday night. Colt Emerson drove in two of Seattle's three first-inning runs. The Mariners have been one of the league's quiet contenders in the AL West, and Woo continues to be a cornerstone of a rotation that is pitching well through the first half.

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
Bryan Woo last night vs. Baltimore:

7 IP. 0 ER. 3 H. 5 K.

Mariners 3-0 Orioles. Colt Emerson drove in 2 in the first inning.

Seattle is quietly building something. #Mariners

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text:
Mariners starters since the All-Star break last year have been one of the better kept secrets in baseball.

Bryan Woo last night: 7 shutout innings, 3 hits allowed.

Seattle is 1st-half sneaky. #Mariners

article.headline: Bryan Woo's Gem Leads Mariners Past Orioles 3-0

stories.title: All-Star Ballot: Ohtani at 1.165M Votes, Phase 1 Closes June 25
stories.angle: Shohei Ohtani leads all of baseball with 1,165,133 votes, on pace to earn an automatic starting spot as the AL designated hitter when Phase 1 closes June 25. Yordan Alvarez paces AL DH vote at 1,015,768 — this is Ohtani leading all players total, not Alvarez leading AL. Aaron Judge third at 977,460. Schwarber leads NL. MLB All-Star Game July 14 at Citizens Bank Park in Philadelphia. Six days until voting Phase 1 closes.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
2026 MLB All-Star ballot — 6 days left in Phase 1 (closes June 25):

🏆 Ohtani: 1,165,133 votes — leads all of baseball
🏆 Yordan Alvarez: 1,015,768 — leads AL
🏆 Aaron Judge: 977,460

Game: July 14, Citizens Bank Park, Philadelphia.

Who are you voting for? #MLB #AllStarGame

article.headline: All-Star Ballot Update: Ohtani Leads All of Baseball, Phase 1 Closes June 25

stories.title: Thursday Night Roundup — Royals 14-6, Woo Shuts Down O's, Witt's Night
stories.angle: Thursday June 18 results: Royals 14-6 Cardinals (Witt HR then exits with knee, club-record 5 doubles in a 6-run second inning), Mariners 3-0 Orioles (Woo gem), Guardians and Brewers both active. Full evening recap.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Thursday MLB results:

Royals 14, Cardinals 6 — Witt HR, then exits with knee (MRI pending)
Mariners 3, Orioles 0 — Woo 7 IP, 0 ER

Club-record 5 doubles for KC in a 6-run 2nd inning.

The win almost felt secondary. #Royals #Mariners

article.headline: Thursday Night Roundup: Royals 14-6, Mariners 3-0, and the Night Witt Went Down
